content_hints.description: The back room of The Cracked Lens. Sara unlocks a door that has not been opened in years. Behind it, a workshop frozen in time -- her grandfather's private sanctum. Dust sheets cover the workbenches. The air is stale and dry, preserved like the inside of a tomb. A wooden crate sits on the central table, its lid pried off long ago. Inside: velvet lining shaped to hold a camera. The camera is gone -- sold to Oscar -- but the velvet still hums with residual bleed.
content_hints.read_aloud: The box is lined with lead. You can feel the residual bleed before you touch it -- a pins-and-needles sensation crawling up your wrists. The velvet interior still holds the impression of the camera, and around that impression, Sara's grandfather scratched symbols into the lead with a nail. Wards. Containment sigils. He knew what the camera was, and he built a cage for it.
event_triggers: The lead-lined box contained the Eye of Emrys. Grandfather's containment wards are scratched into the lead. The camera was sealed deliberately.

loot.name: Grandfather's Lead-Lined Box
loot.description: The original containment vessel for the Eye of Emrys. Lead-lined with hand-scratched ward symbols. Could potentially be used to contain the camera again -- or to shield against its effects during use.

content_hints.description: A roll-top desk against the far wall, its cubbyholes stuffed with papers in three languages. Sara's grandfather was meticulous. Among the papers: a blueprint for the Eye of Emrys, drawn in ink on vellum. The camera's internal mechanism includes a chamber lined with a material that does not exist in any engineering manual. Beside the blueprint, a journal entry in shaking handwriting.
content_hints.read_aloud: The journal is written in a hand that starts steady and ends in a tremor. The final relevant passage reads: 'Father Vienno was dead three days when I turned the Eye upon him. He sat up. He reached for me. His eyes were alive but his flesh was still cold. I sealed the camera and I will never use it again. The Eye does not merely capture the dead -- it wakes them. But if it can wake them, it can also return them to sleep. The process is reversible. It must be. The alternative is unthinkable.'
event_triggers: REVEAL: The Eye of Emrys can both reanimate the dead AND return them to their natural state. The process is reversible. The camera is the weapon and the cure.
Grandfather's notes describe the camera's bleed-infused lens chamber. Each exposure consumes a glass plate. The camera has a limited number of plates remaining.

loot.name: Eye of Emrys Blueprint
loot.description: A detailed technical drawing of the camera's internal mechanism. The lens chamber contains a material annotated as 'vitrified bleed precipitate.' The blueprint shows how to load and aim the camera for maximum effect -- and how to reverse the process.

content_hints.investigation_success: Hidden beneath the desk's false bottom: three unused glass plates for the Eye of Emrys, wrapped in lead foil. Each plate can capture -- or release -- one subject. Only three shots remain.
